title: Thanatos interlude
wikitext:
[]: Thanatos interlude             Quote Post by Crazy Packers Fan » Fri Aug 22, 2008 7:58 am In the song Thanatos - If I Can't Be Yours- there's a short part in the middle with background singing; it seems to be the same line sung twice in a row. This is also its own track from Evangelion-VOX, called interlude-Thanatos. My question is if anyone knows what is being sung there- is it actually something in Japanese, or simply a couple lines of jibberish? I know it's not especially important, but I wonder why it would be included as its own track if it had no significance.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 22, 2008 10:03 am To be honest, I always thought that interlude was something in an African language if we're thinking of the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Fri Aug 22, 2008 3:57 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: To be honest, I always thought that interlude was something in an African language if we're thinking of the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by The Lincolnshire Poacher » Fri Aug 22, 2008 7:02 pm The instrumental interlude with the chants do sound African-influenced, this effect could be intentional or not- considering 'Thanatos- If I Can't Be Yours' obviously has musical components prominent in African pop music: jazz breaks and R&B. But I am convinced the chants themselves don't actually mean anything, or for that matter belong to any language.

Article: Thanatos interlude
[]: Thanatos interlude             Quote Post by Crazy Packers Fan » Fri Aug 22, 2008 7:58 am In the song Thanatos - If I Can't Be Yours- there's a short part in the middle with background singing; it seems to be the same line sung twice in a row. This is also its own track from Evangelion-VOX, called interlude-Thanatos. My question is if anyone knows what is being sung there- is it actually something in Japanese, or simply a couple lines of jibberish? I know it's not especially important, but I wonder why it would be included as its own track if it had no significance.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 22, 2008 10:03 am To be honest, I always thought that interlude was something in an African language if we're thinking of the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Fri Aug 22, 2008 3:57 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: To be honest, I always thought that interlude was something in an African language if we're thinking of the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by The Lincolnshire Poacher » Fri Aug 22, 2008 7:02 pm The instrumental interlude with the chants do sound African-influenced, this effect could be intentional or not- considering 'Thanatos- If I Can't Be Yours' obviously has musical components prominent in African pop music: jazz breaks and R&B. But I am convinced the chants themselves don't actually mean anything, or for that matter belong to any language.
